notes: EDI enablement client; full send/receive EDI + EFT; $130 license fee; 16-bit Win95/3.1
Free receive-only EDI forms product; entry-level for spoke organizations
ACH bank-side payment processing client; Windows NT
Traditional VAN model charged $1-$5/transaction; still used in healthcare/retail but declining vs AS2/API
EC Network ran as private extranet on UUNET; acquired by MCI WorldCom 1996
EC Network hub ran on redundant Windows NT with MS SQL Server 6.5
Database backend for EC Network hub; EC Start used MS Access locally
Session security for EC Network; symmetric session keys; presaged SSL/TLS adoption
[post-publication technology] RFC 4130 AS2 standard (2005) ultimately fulfilled the EC Company's vision of internet-native EDI
